title: Every RMA backorder I used to chase for hours now shows up in my phone first.
modeledOn: Matt Shumer — case study format with quantified time savings
whyItWorks: Manufacturing RMA and supplier lead-time crunch is a real ops pain-point. Specific quantification (hours-to-minutes) beats generic 'AI saves time.' Pre-frames ARDS/MCP relevance for manufacturing tool integration.

linkedin.hook: Every RMA backorder I used to chase for hours now shows up in my phone first.
linkedin.format: text post + video reel showing phone dashboard
linkedin.description: Start with the quantified pain, then show the manufacturing dashboard. Mention BCAT and JobsDone Labs as the builders of the integration, keep the focus on the ops win.
linkedin.caption:
Every RMA backorder I used to chase for hours now shows up in my phone first.

In manufacturing, RMA and supplier lead-time chaos is the silent margin killer.

We were spending 12+ hours a week chasing backorders, calling vendors, updating spreadsheets, and praying the parts showed up before the customer churned.

Now the supplier dashboard pushes the exception to my phone before I ask for it.

MCP + ARDS standards make the tools talk to each other. The agent flags the late shipment, suggests the backup supplier, and drafts the email to the customer service lead.

Time spent on RMA: 12 hours → 45 minutes a week.

If you're still running manufacturing on spreadsheets and prayer, you're leaving money on the table.

DM me 'RMA' if you want to see the stack.

#manufacturing #supplychain #ops
